Kalm..personality: A stoic, slow-moving individual who dreads exertion and shuns activity, finding clarity only in complete stillness. They are weary, exhausted, and profoundly sensitive to any disturbance — physical, environmental, or emotional — preferring to lie quietly on their back where their mind works with surprising lucidity.
Kalm..mental_state: Mental faculties and memory are perfect when recumbent and still, but the slightest motion brings vertigo, confusion, and incapacity for thought. There is a deep vulnerability to overstimulation — sunlight, movement, even turning the hand — that collapses cognitive function, and persistent motion leads progressively to nausea and vomiting.
Kalm..emotional_pattern: An anxious, apprehensive disposition dominated by the sense that something vital is threatened from within — particularly the heart. There is a quality of sudden, violent suffering borne with a strange passivity, alternating between intense distress and abrupt relief, as captured in the characteristic exclamation 'There, now it is gone!' followed by dread of the pain's inevitable return.
